LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS TAD NGUYEN, AND, KIM NGUYEN DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER TO 5TH AND 6TH CAUSES OF ACTION SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. NO EXTREME OR OUTRAGEOUS CONDUCT ALLEGED. NO PATTERN OF CONTINUOUS ENCROACHMENT WITH 23 YEAR GAP BETWEEN FIRST AND SECOND ALLEGED INCIDENTS. NO DEMONSTRATION OF MALICIOUS CONDUCT. NO REASONABLE PROBABILITY THAT DEFECT CAN BE CURED BY AMENDMENT. (MOVING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-17-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS TAD NGUYEN, and, KIM NGUYEN MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion is granted without leave to amend for reasons stated in the ruling on demurrer. (Moving party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-17-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS TAD NGUYEN, and, KIM NGUYEN DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer to 5th and 6th causes of action sustained without leave to amend. No extreme or outrageous conduct alleged. No pattern of continuous encroachment with 23 year gap between first and second alleged incidents. No demonstration of malicious conduct. No reasonable probability that defect can be cured by amendment. (Moving party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
